title: Enki Cognitive-Core Boundary and Generic Contracts
description: Establish the product-neutral Enki bounded context, stable generic contracts, version compatibility rules, and uncertainty-preserving confidence behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Enki core remains independent of downstream product, Person Object, Organization Object, EOA/V8, clinical, maturity, and publication ontologies
Human objectives, priorities, values, choices, accountability, and outcomes remain external authority
Generic contracts and ports preserve evidence, temporal applicability, context, confidence, lineage, findings, and disclosure boundaries
Contract versions resolve to exact, backward-compatible, explicitly forward-compatible, unsupported, or ambiguous outcomes
Unsupported and ambiguous versions fail closed
UNKNOWN confidence is deferred and non-UNKNOWN confidence requires attributable known evidence

evidence.description: Versioning, confidence-use, and canonical evidence hardening.
